summary: Excellent front‑of‑house audience recording captures a scorching 1985 show highlighted by a blistering opener, haunting "He's Gone > Miracle" sequence and an unforgettable Day Tripper.
review: The tape delivers a crystal‑clear audience source with Phil’s bass sitting prominently in the mix while retaining the live vibe. Musically the concert shines from the powerhouse opener through a standout second set featuring a haunting "He's Gone > Miracle" segue, a tight Jerry‑and‑drums jam and a wild Day Tripper, making it one of the year’s best performances.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviewers praise both the musical performance and the audience‑source recording; average star rating is 4.5 with consistent positive language.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Praised as the best audience (FOB) source, very clear and balanced with prominent Phil bass and minimal crowd bleed; captures live feel without excessive noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, energetic opener sets the tone; first set solid though not uniformly exciting, while the second set picks up momentum with a haunting "He's Gone > Miracle" sequence, tight jam sections and an exuberant Day Tripper.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His playing remains solid despite a cold he was battling; several reviewers note his tone and the memorable Jerry‑and‑drums jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is very prominent in the mix, giving Phil a standout presence throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers (Bill & Mickey) are highlighted during the extended jam with Jerry, delivering a powerful rhythmic foundation.
